title: Vincetoxicum rotundifolium
taxonomy: Vincetoxicum rotundifolium belongs to the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It is a species of flowering plant, and its classification is further categorized under the Asclepiadoideae subfamily. This subfamily is part of the Gentianales order, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the Apocynaceae family. The classification of Vincetoxicum rotundifolium is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids. The plant's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
uses: Vincetoxicum rotundifolium, a plant known for its various medicinal properties, has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat different health conditions. Its root and leaves are rich in bioactive compounds, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory diseases. Some studies suggest that Vincetoxicum rotundifolium may also have potential in the treatment of cancer and cardiovascular diseases. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for medicinal use.

It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce pain and improve overall health. constituents: Vincetoxicum rotundifolium contains several active constituents, including vincetoxicine, vincamine, and vincine. These compounds are known to have various biological activities, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Vincetoxicine has been reported to have a potential role in the treatment of cardiovascular diseases. Vincamine is a vasodilator that can help to improve blood circulation. Additionally, vincine has been found to have antimicrobial properties.
parts: Vincetoxicum rotundifolium is known to have medicinal properties. The plant's roots and leaves are used in traditional medicine. Vincetoxicum rotundifolium is said to have antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce fever and relieve pain. side_effects: Vincetoxicum rotundifolium is a plant that should be handled with caution, as it may cause gastrointestinal side effects such as nausea and vomiting if ingested. The plant's toxic compounds can also lead to cardiovascular issues, including high blood pressure and palpitations. In some cases, exposure to Vincetoxicum rotundifolium may cause skin irritation and allergic reactions. It is essential to note that the side effects of Vincetoxicum rotundifolium may vary depending on the individual and the method of exposure. As with any toxic plant, it is crucial to exercise extreme caution when handling or approaching Vincetoxicum rotundifolium.
intro: Vincetoxicum rotundifolium, also known as Round-leaved Periploca or Round-leaved Swallowwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The health benefits of Vincetoxicum rotundifolium include its use in traditional medicine to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and inflammatory diseases, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include vincamine, vinconine, and periplocin, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Vincetoxicum rotundifolium is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, often combined with other herbs, to promote cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress.
